componentDescription: Snackbar displays temporary notifications that auto-dismiss after a brief period. Use for non-critical, transient feedback: 'Saved', 'Copied to clipboard', 'Email sent'. For persistent important messages requiring user attention, use Alert instead. Snackbar is typically invoked via a toast utility function rather than rendered directly.
features: Multiple semantic variants matching Alert variants
Optional action button within the notification

usageExamples.title: Success notification after save
usageExamples.description: Brief success confirmation after a save operation.

usageExamples.title: Snackbar with undo action
usageExamples.description: Notification with an undo action button for reversible operations.

usageExamples.title: Direct Snackbar rendering
usageExamples.description: Rendering Snackbar as a component when not using a toast utility.
usageExamples.code:
<Snackbar
  message="File uploaded successfully"
  variant="success"
  duration={4000}
  position="bottom-right"
  onClose={() => setShowSnackbar(false)}
/>

message.llmContext: Required. The notification text. Keep it under 80 characters. Use past tense for completed actions ('Saved', 'Deleted') and present tense for ongoing states. Avoid technical jargon.

variant.llmContext: Choose based on the type of feedback: 'success' for completed actions (save, copy, send), 'error' for failed actions, 'warning' for important cautions, 'neutral' for informational messages. Avoid 'error' for snackbar — use Alert for persistent errors instead.
variant.propDescription: Semantic variant controlling color and icon

action.llmContext: Optional action button inside the snackbar. Pass { label: string, onClick: () => void }. Best used for reversible actions ('Undo', 'View'). Keep label short (1-2 words). Providing an action implicitly extends the duration so users have time to click it.
action.propDescription: Optional action button shown in the snackbar

duration.llmContext: Milliseconds before auto-dismiss. Default is 3000ms (3 seconds). Use 5000ms when an action button is present. Use 2000ms for simple confirmations like 'Copied'. Do not set very long durations — use Alert for persistent messages.

position.llmContext: 'bottom-right' is the most common position. Use 'bottom-center' for mobile-first UIs. Avoid 'top-*' positions as they conflict with navigation bars and alerts.
position.propDescription: Screen position where the snackbar appears
